title: The Test
content:
(Musa loquitur.)

    I hung my verses in the wind,
    Time and tide their faults may find.
    All were winnowed through and through,
    Five lines lasted sound and true;
    Five were smelted in a pot
    Than the South more fierce and hot;
    These the siroc could not melt,
    Fire their fiercer flaming felt,
    And the meaning was more white
    Than July's meridian light.
    Sunshine cannot bleach the snow,
    Nor time unmake what poets know.
    Have you eyes to find the five
    Which five hundred did survive?
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Ralph Waldo Emerson delivers a powerful performance in "The Test"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
